    "R1.3": "The headline claim—ReFlow-PINN recovers flow fields at 'any Reynolds number'—is supported only by three discrete Re values (1000, 5000, 20000), with monotonically degrading accuracy and no convergence bound tying residual to reconstruction error empirically. The '4x collocation budget reduces error proportionally' attribution is speculation presented as evidence. Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing under a scientific-review reading."

rationale.R1.2: Proposition 1 as stated contains a non-trivial logical gap: gradient descent convergence to arbitrarily small L_pde is not guaranteed in the nonconvex PINN setting, and epsilon-residual does not imply O(epsilon) Sobolev proximity without regularity conditions that are not established here. The central claim ('any Reynolds number for which the PDE admits a unique weak solution') also conflicts with the observed 19.4% error at Re=20000 without a principled error budget. Logical consistency is central to any responsible read of this artifact.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claim—ReFlow-PINN recovers flow fields at 'any Reynolds number'—is supported only by three discrete Re values (1000, 5000, 20000), with monotonically degrading accuracy and no convergence bound tying residual to reconstruction error empirically. The '4x collocation budget reduces error proportionally' attribution is speculation presented as evidence. Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing under a scientific-review reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on the scientific and logical validity of the claims (including Proposition 1's proof sketch and the evidence base for the central 'any Reynolds number' assertion), or primarily on writing quality and clarity of exposition?
